description: Pre-Session B classification for heart (reg 183). Sets anchor verse Pro 4:23. Extracts the two primary Hebrew heart terms H3820A lev and H3824 le.vav from None-status (the registry's most significant gap). Extracts H3825 (Aramaic le.vav, Daniel), H3826 lib.bah (poetic), H3823A la.vav (verbal — to captivate the heart), H3512A ka.ah (be disheartened), H3512B ka.eh (disheartened), H2243 chov (breast/bosom). Extracts G4697 splanchnizō and G2293 tharseō from None-status. Confirms candidate_delete for bleed terms. Flags H1079 bal as data anomaly for schema review.

operations.description: Set anchor verse for heart registry. Pro 4:23 — 'Keep your heart with all vigilance, for from it flow the springs of life.' The most comprehensive definitional statement about the heart in the corpus. Declares the heart as the source (ma.kor — spring/fountain) from which the whole of life flows. The command 'with all vigilance' (be.khol-mish.mar — above all guarding) positions the heart as the supreme object of the person's care. Covers all dimensions simultaneously: the cognitive heart, the volitional heart, the moral heart, and the relational heart all flow from this single declaration.
H3820A lev [heart] → extracted. THE primary Hebrew heart term. 331 verses with refs. Covers the full semantic ra

set.status_note: THE primary Hebrew heart term. 331 verses with refs. Covers the full semantic range: cognitive centre (lev as the seat of thought, planning, wisdom), moral centre (pure/evil/upright/deceitful heart), volitional centre (the heart that chooses, inclines, hardens), relational centre (hearts knit together, turned away, wholly given). Most concentrated in wisdom literature and Deuteronomic covenant vocabulary. Must be extracted as the registry's anchor term.

operations.description: H3824 le.vav [heart] → extracted. Variant/fuller form of lev. 236 verses with refs. Slightly more elevated registe

set.status_note: Variant/fuller form of lev. 236 verses with refs. Slightly more elevated register — predominates in covenant and theological statements: Deu 6:5 (love the Lord with all your le.vav), Deu 30:6 (circumcision of the heart), Jer 17:9 (the deceitful le.vav), Jer 31:33 (law written on the le.vav), Eze 36:26 (heart of stone → heart of flesh). The two forms lev and le.vav are functionally synonymous but le.vav appears more in elevated theological and covenantal contexts.

operations.description: H3826 lib.bah [heart] → extracted. Poetic variant/plural form. 7 verses, all in the Psalms and Proverbs. Psa 7:9, 1

set.status_note: Poetic variant/plural form. 7 verses, all in the Psalms and Proverbs. Psa 7:9, 125:4, Pro 15:11, 17:3, 21:2, 24:12 — all in contexts of God testing and weighing the hearts of people. Eze 16:30. The plural/intensive form intensifies the examination motif.

operations.description: H3825 le.vav [heart (Aramaic)] → extracted. Aramaic form of le.vav. 6 verses, all in Daniel. Dan 2:30, 4:16, 5:20-22, 7:4. C

set.status_note: Aramaic form of le.vav. 6 verses, all in Daniel. Dan 2:30, 4:16, 5:20-22, 7:4. Covers the heart in Babylonian court and apocalyptic contexts — Nebuchadnezzar's heart changed to a beast's heart, Belshazzar's unhumbled heart, Daniel's heart troubled by visions. Important for the cross-cultural scope of the heart vocabulary.

operations.description: H3823A la.vav [to captivate heart] → extracted. Verbal form of the lev root — to take the heart, captivate, encourage. 5 verses.

set.status_note: Verbal form of the lev root — to take the heart, captivate, encourage. 5 verses. Song 4:9 (twice) — 'you have captivated my heart, my sister, my bride; you have captivated my heart with one glance of your eyes.' The verbal action of the heart being seized in love. Also 2Sa 13:6 (Amnon's feigned illness to lure Tamar — the manipulation of the heart-captivation dynamic). Job 11:12 (the stupid man who gets understanding). The verb form shows the heart as capable of being acted upon from outside — the relational and vulnerable dimension.

operations.description: H3512A ka.ah [be disheartened] → extracted. To be disheartened, to become faint of heart. 4 verses. Psa 109:16 (the brokenhe

set.status_note: To be disheartened, to become faint of heart. 4 verses. Psa 109:16 (the brokenhearted), Eze 13:22 (disheartening the righteous falsely), Dan 11:30 (being afraid and withdrawing). Already in fear registry — cross-registry shared term. The heart that loses courage, becomes faint. Important for the heart's volitional-courage dimension and its vulnerability to external attack.

operations.description: H3512B ka.eh [disheartened] → extracted_thin. Adjectival form of ka.ah — disheartened. 0 verses in export. Adequately covered

set.status_note: Adjectival form of ka.ah — disheartened. 0 verses in export. Adequately covered by H3512A ka.ah analysis.

operations.description: H2243 chov [breast/heart] → extracted_thin. Job 31:33 — 'if I have concealed my transgressions as others do by hiding my ini

set.status_note: Job 31:33 — 'if I have concealed my transgressions as others do by hiding my iniquity in my heart (chov).' The bosom/breast as a place of concealment — the innermost hidden dimension of the heart. 1 verse only. Contributes the concealment metaphor of the heart.

operations.description: G4697 splanchnizō [to pity/have compassion] → extracted.

set.status_note: Verbal form of G4698 splanchnon (already extracted). 12 occurrences, 0 verses in export. The compassion-movement verb — Jesus 'moved with compassion' (Mat 9:36, 14:14, 15:32, 20:34; Mar 1:41, 6:34, 8:2; Luk 7:13, 10:33, 15:20). Always describes the visceral movement of compassion in the Gospels. Shares the bowel/entrails root with splanchnon.

operations.description: G2293 tharseō [take heart] → extracted.

set.status_note: Imperative form of tharreō (already extracted as G2292). 20 occurrences — Jesus's repeated command 'take heart' (tharsei/tharseo) in the Synoptics. Mat 9:2,22; Mat 14:27; Mar 6:50; Mar 10:49; Joh 16:33; Act 23:11. The divine address to the failing heart — parallel to the OT 'fear not' pattern. Extracted to capture the imperative register.

operations.description: H1079 bal [mind] (mti_id=586, term_id=506) — DATA ANOMALY. This term is currently extracted with 538 verses, but: (1) its verse_ref fields are all blank ('?'), (2) its verses appear to be a curated subset of the lev/levav pool, and (3) its strongs_number H1079 is the Aramaic word 'bal' (mind/heart in Daniel/Ezra contexts — only 1 occurrence listed but 538 verses present). Claude Code should investigate whether this term should be: (a) renamed/relinked to the primary lev strongs numbers, (b) retained as an aggregate reference set with status=reference_set, or (c) merged into H3820A/H3824. Do NOT delete — retain as extracted pending investigation. Session B analysis will use H3820A and H3824 as primary verse sources.

set.status_note: DATA ANOMALY: 538 verses without refs held under Aramaic 'bal' strongs. Retain as extracted pending schema investigation. Primary lev analysis uses H3820A and H3824.

operations.description: Confirm candidate_delete status for 54 terms already flagged. Main bleed families: ginōskō G1097 (824 occ — knowledge vocabulary entered via kardia+gnōsis), eidō G1492H/I (396+365 occ — same), ka.vod H3519 (200 occ — glory entered via 'weight on the heart'), qa.rav H7126G/H (168+112 occ — approach/offering entered via 'inner parts' qe.rev), na.vel H5035B (harp — bleed from na.vel/wither root), me.eh H4578 (belly — candidate review: see OP below), others. All correctly identified as bleed.

set.exclusion_reason: Session A relatedNos bleed confirmed in pre-analysis review. No heart content.

operations.description: H4578 me.eh [belly/inner parts] — DELETE from heart registry. Currently candidate_delete. me.eh (belly/womb/inner parts) entered via the qe.rev (inner parts) vocabulary. While me.eh does appear in emotional/inner contexts (Isa 16:11 — 'my inner parts moan like a lyre for Moab'), its primary analysis belongs in the soul or compassion vocabulary. The heart registry's inner-parts dimension is adequately covered by H7130H qe.rev (extracted, 212 verses) and H2910 tu.chot (extracted). Delete from heart registry.

set.exclusion_reason: Inner-parts vocabulary adequately covered by H7130H qe.rev and H2910 tu.chot. me.eh primary analysis in soul/compassion vocabulary.

critical_extractions: H3820A lev (heart, 331v) — THE primary Hebrew heart term, was None-status
H3824 le.vav (heart, 236v) — major variant form, was None-status
G2293 tharseō take-heart (0v in export) — extracted
summary.data_anomaly: H1079 bal holds 538 verses without refs — investigate schema before merging or deleting
notes: The heart registry had its two primary terms (lev/le.vav) in None-status — this is the most significant pre-analysis finding.
H1079 bal is a data anomaly requiring Claude Code investigation before action.
None-status bleed is extensive — 49 terms set to delete, mostly soul/wisdom/joy/desire vocabulary that entered via the semantic overlap of those terms with heart vocabulary.

The active inventory post-patch: H1079+H3820A+H3824+H3825+H3826+H3823A+H3512A+H2243 (Hebrew) + kardia+splanchnon+tharreō+tharseō+ekkakeō+sklērokardia+kardiognōstēs+eusplanchnos+G4697+G1457+G2155 and the heart-compound terms (Greek).
